name: St. Jean'S
description_short: St. Jean's Credit Union is a federally insured state-chartered credit union headquartered in Lynn, MA, serving 15,417 members across 4 locations since 1910.
description_long:
St. Jean's Credit Union was established on September 29, 1910, and has operated continuously for over a century as a member-owned financial cooperative. Headquartered in Lynn, Massachusetts, the credit union maintains $158.9 million in total assets and serves approximately 15,417 members through a network of 4 branch locations across Massachusetts (Lynn, Newburyport, Revere, and Salem). As a federally insured state-chartered credit union, deposits are protected by NCUA insurance, and the institution maintains membership in the Federal Home Loan Bank system.

St. Jean's offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business banking services. Member products include no-cost share drafts (checking accounts), share certificates with low minimum balance requirements, and various loan products spanning real estate loans, business loans, consumer loans, and overdraft protection. Electronic services are robust, featuring internet home banking, mobile banking, ATM access with no surcharge options, bill payment, remote deposit capture, and e-statements. The credit union also provides mortgage processing services as an approved mortgage seller and offers brokered deposits and certificates of deposit for members seeking higher yields.

The institution distinguishes itself through specific member-focused offerings not common at all credit unions. St. Jean's holds a Low Income Designation, indicating commitment to serving economically disadvantaged members. The credit union provides financial education services including financial counseling, financial literacy workshops, and first-time homebuyer programs. Bilingual services are available to Spanish-speaking members, and the organization offers no-cost bill payment and insurance/investment sales services. The long operational history since 1910 reflects institutional stability and deep community roots.

St. Jean's represents a solid, traditional credit union option for Massachusetts residents seeking member-owned banking with modest asset size and community focus. The 4-location footprint limits convenience for those outside the Lynn, Newburyport, Revere, and Salem areas. As a smaller institution ($159M assets), it may offer fewer specialized products or competitive rates than larger regional credit unions or banks, though the specific APRs and fees are not disclosed on the available website content. The institution appears well-regulated and appropriately capitalized, making it suitable for straightforward banking needs rather than complex financial services. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Share certificates with low minimum balance requirements
Real estate and mortgage loans with mortgage processing
Business loans and business share accounts
Consumer loans, indirect consumer loans, and micro consumer loans
Overdraft protection and courtesy pay services
Internet home banking and mobile banking applications
No-surcharge ATM access and ATM/debit card program
Remote deposit capture and electronic bill payment
Financial counseling and financial literacy workshops
Brokered deposits and brokered certificates of deposit
pros: NCUA deposit insurance protection for member accounts
No-cost share drafts (checking accounts) with no monthly fees
No-surcharge ATM network access for member convenience
Low Income Designation indicates commitment to underserved communities
Free financial counseling and first-time homebuyer program support
Bilingual services available for Spanish-speaking members
Member-owned cooperative structure with 15,417 active members
Federal Home Loan Bank membership providing additional stability
cons: Only 4 branch locations across Massachusetts limits geographic accessibility
Smaller asset base ($159M) may result in fewer competitive rates or specialized loan products compared to larger institutions
Website provides no disclosure of specific APRs, fees, or rate comparisons with competitors
No information about online-only account opening or whether membership is restricted to geographic area
Limited information available about customer service hours or digital banking feature depth
best_for: Massachusetts residents in Lynn, Newburyport, Revere, or Salem seeking traditional credit union banking
Low-income individuals and families seeking member-owned banking with financial education support
First-time homebuyers interested in mortgage programs and financial counseling
Spanish-speaking members seeking bilingual banking services in Massachusetts
diagnosis: St. Jean's Credit Union is best for Massachusetts residents with ties to its 4-branch service area (Lynn, Newburyport, Revere, Salem) who value member-owned banking, community focus, and free or low-cost services. The main caveat is that smaller asset size and limited branch network may restrict product availability and convenience compared to larger regional institutions—potential members should verify rates and fees align with their needs.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
